vibration_standards.text: ISO 10816-3 evaluates machine vibration by measuring RMS velocity (mm/s) on bearing housings. For medium machines 15 kW to 300 kW (Class II), zone boundaries are: Zone A (good) up to 1.4 mm/s, Zone B (acceptable, unrestricted operation) 1.4-2.8 mm/s, Zone C (unsatisfactory, restricted operation) 2.8-4.5 mm/s, Zone D (unacceptable, damage may occur) above 4.5 mm/s.

For large machines 300 kW to 50 MW on rigid foundations (Class III) and flexible foundations (Class IV), the unsatisfactory threshold rises. Class III: Zone B up to 4.5 mm/s, Zone C up to 7.1 mm/s, Zone D above 7.1 mm/s. Class IV: Zone B up to 7.1 mm/s, Zone C up to 11.0 mm/s, Zone D above 11.0 mm/s.

A sustained increase of 25% or more in RMS vibration velocity from an established baseline indicates a developing fault even when absolute values remain within the acceptable zone. Trend monitoring is recommended alongside absolute limits.

Vibration concentrated at 1x running speed typically indicates unbalance. Vibration at 2x line frequency in motors suggests electrical faults or stator issues. Bearing defects produce high-frequency vibration at characteristic defect frequencies (BPFO, BPFI). Gear faults appear at gear-mesh frequency with sidebands.

manuals.text: Centrifugal pump drive-end bearings should run below 75 C. Bearing temperature rise combined with vibration at 1x RPM and axial play indicates outer race wear. Replace bearings as a set and verify shaft alignment within 0.05 mm before restart.

Cavitation presents as broadband random vibration and a gravel-like noise with fluctuating discharge pressure. Restore net positive suction head (NPSH) by clearing suction strainers and verifying suction valve is fully open. Prolonged cavitation erodes the impeller.

Induction motor stator winding temperature must stay below 85 C for Class B insulation. Vibration at twice line frequency with overheating suggests winding insulation degradation. Perform megger and surge tests; rewind if insulation resistance falls below 1 MOhm/kV.

manuals.source: Atlas Copco GA compressor manual §4 Oil system
manuals.text: Rising discharge temperature with oil carryover on a screw compressor points to a clogged oil separator element. Replace the separator element and oil/filters together. Knocking with metal particles in the oil indicates airend rotor bearing wear requiring overhaul.

Industrial fan vibration at 1x RPM is usually aerodynamic unbalance from dust build-up or blade erosion. Clean the impeller and re-balance to ISO 1940 G2.5. Inspect blades for cracking before returning to service.

manuals.source: SEW gearbox manual §7 Gears and bearings
manuals.text: Gearbox faults appear at gear-mesh frequency with sidebands; tooth pitting raises vibration and operating temperature. Bearing spalling shows energy at bearing defect frequencies with oil contamination. Replace affected gears or bearings and refill with EP gear oil.

maintenance_procedures.text: Bearing replacement: isolate and lock out machine, remove coupling, extract worn bearing with puller, inspect shaft journal, install new bearing with induction heater, re-grease to NLGI 2, verify alignment, run-in at low load. Estimated downtime 6 hours.

maintenance_procedures.fault: Cavitation due to low suction pressure
maintenance_procedures.text: Cavitation remediation: clean suction strainer, confirm suction valve fully open, verify tank level and NPSH margin, throttle discharge to restore duty point. Estimated downtime 3 hours.

Stator rewind: de-energize and verify zero energy, remove rotor, strip damaged windings, rewind to spec, vacuum-pressure impregnate varnish, megger test, reassemble and improve cooling airflow. Estimated downtime 24 hours.

Field balancing: mount accelerometers and phase reference, measure 1x amplitude and phase, calculate trial and correction weights, attach correction mass, verify residual unbalance within ISO 1940 G2.5, torque foundation bolts. Estimated downtime 4 hours.
Separator service: depressurize air receiver, replace oil separator element and O-rings, change compressor oil and oil filter, restart and check for oil carryover. Estimated downtime 5 hours.

Gear overhaul: drain lubricant, open inspection cover, assess gear contact pattern, replace worn gear set, check backlash, refill with EP gear oil, run-in under monitored load. Estimated downtime 20 hours.
